CentralDeploySettings.description:
Central-deploy roster and component-access settings.

Provides the base URL and bearer token for the central-deploy
management-plane API.  At session start the agent fetches the
``GET /chat/components`` roster (a list of component agents the chat
is allowed to call), caches it with a short TTL, and loads each
component's declared skill into the agent.

Attributes:
    url: Base URL of the central-deploy API (no trailing slash).
    api_token: Bearer token for authenticating to the central-deploy
        API.  Required when any component access is expected.
    roster_cache_ttl: Seconds to cache the roster before re-fetching.
        Default 300 (5 min).
    component_response_max_chars: Truncation limit for GET/HEAD component
        responses — write methods keep the existing 8,000 limit. This lets
        the agent enumerate large ticket lists (e.g. mill board blocked
        tickets) without truncation.
CentralDeploySettings.title: CentralDeploySettings

ComponentClientSettings.description:
Component client settings.

Provides the per-request timeout and a list of remote component agents
that the chat agent can inspect and configure (monitor / config-get /
config-set).  Gated behind ``enabled`` — when False (the default), no
component-client tools are offered to the agent.
ComponentClientSettings.title: ComponentClientSettings

ComponentTarget.description:
A single remote component agent target.

Attributes:
    base_url: Base URL of the component agent's HTTP API (no trailing
        slash).  Required.
    label: Optional human-readable label shown in the agent's
        component list tool output.  When empty, the base_url is used
        as the display name.
ComponentTarget.title: ComponentTarget

ConversationSettings.description:
Multi-session conversation store settings.

Attributes:
    max_history_turns: Maximum conversation turns to retain per session.
    max_conversations: Maximum concurrent conversations across all owners.
    persist_path: Path to the JSON persistence file on disk.
ConversationSettings.title: ConversationSettings

DiagnosticsSettings.description:
Diagnostics subsystem settings.

Blocked-ticket diagnostics capture, recurrence detection, and
systemic-fix surfacing.  Enabled by default.

Attributes:
    enabled: Master switch.  When False, no diagnostics events are
        captured and no diagnostic tools are offered.
    store_path: Path to the diagnostic-event JSON persistence file.
    proposals_path: Path to the fix-proposal JSON persistence file.
    effectiveness_path: Path to the effectiveness-report JSON
        persistence file.
    recurrence_threshold: Number of occurrences within the
        recurrence window that triggers a recurrence alert.
    recurrence_window_days: Look-back window in days for recurrence
        detection.
    observation_window_days: Days after a fix is applied to wait
        before generating an effectiveness report.
DiagnosticsSettings.title: DiagnosticsSettings

DirectRepoSettings.description:
Direct-repository capability settings.

Push branches and open PRs as the robotsix-mill GitHub App
installation.  Gated behind ``enabled``.

Attributes:
    enabled: Master switch for direct-repo capability.
    github_app_id: GitHub App slug (or numeric id).
    github_app_private_key: RSA private key in PEM format.
    github_app_installation_id: App installation id.
    github_api_base_url: Base URL for GitHub API.
    board_api_base_url: Board HTTP API base URL for ticket-state lookups.
    board_api_token: Optional bearer token for the board API.
    timeout: Per-request HTTP timeout.
DirectRepoSettings.title: DirectRepoSettings

FeedbackSettings.description:
Automated feedback analysis for continuous self-improvement.

When enabled, a feedback run analyses the conversation at compaction
and session-end boundaries, then files improvement tickets via the
board's ``POST /tickets/ingest`` endpoint.  Tickets flow through the
normal human-approval workflow — the feedback run never auto-approves.

Attributes:
    enabled: Master switch.  When ``False``, no feedback runs occur.
    model_level: llmio capability level for the feedback-analysis
        agent (a cheap, single-turn extraction call).  Default ``1``.
    board_url: Base URL of the board HTTP API (no trailing slash).
        Required when *enabled* — the runner POSTs to
        ``{board_url}/tickets/ingest``.
    board_api_token: Optional Bearer token for the board API.
    timeout: Per-request HTTP timeout in seconds for ingest calls.
FeedbackSettings.title: FeedbackSettings

GitHubSecuritySettings.description:
Repository security-feature toggle via the GitHub App installation.

When enabled, the chat agent gains a ``set_repo_security_and_analysis``
tool that can enable or disable repository-level security features
(dependency graph, advanced security, secret scanning) on repos under
the configured GitHub App's installation scope.

**Guardrails built into the tool (not configurable):**
- Repo scope is resolved dynamically from the GitHub App installation
  (list-installation-repositories) — no static allowlist.
- Only repos within the installation scope are modifiable.
- Each feature toggle explicitly requires ``"enabled"`` or ``"disabled"``
  — no accidental bulk changes.

Attributes:
    enabled: Master switch.  When ``False``, no security-feature tool
        is offered.
    github_org: GitHub organisation name whose repos are in scope
        (e.g. ``"damien-robotsix"``).  The tool only targets repos
        under this org.
    deploy_api_key: API key that clients must present in the
        ``X-API-Key`` header when calling the
        ``PATCH /chat/github/repos/{owner}/{repo}/settings``
        endpoint.  When empty, the endpoint returns 503 (unconfigured).
    timeout: Per-request HTTP timeout in seconds.

Note: GitHub App authentication is delegated to
:class:`DirectRepoSettings` — those credentials must also be configured
for the tool to function.
GitHubSecuritySettings.title: GitHubSecuritySettings

KnowledgeSettings.description:
Writable knowledge-base settings.

A plain JSON file on disk that the agent can read, write, and
maintain as a durable store across sessions.

Attributes:
    enabled: Master switch.
    path: Path to the JSON persistence file on disk.
KnowledgeSettings.title: KnowledgeSettings

LangfuseSettings.description:
Langfuse observability settings.

Provides credentials for an OpenTelemetry-backed Langfuse project.
These are used to trace the main chat agent (and subsession agents
spawned from it).  A **separate** ``memory.langfuse`` key set traces
the cognee / LiteLLM pipeline.

When the public key is set, credentials are exported to process-level
environment variables at startup so ``robotsix-llmio``'s OTLP exporter
picks them up automatically.

Attributes:
    public_key: Langfuse project public key.
    secret_key: Langfuse project secret key.
    host: Langfuse host URL (e.g. ``https://cloud.langfuse.com``
        or a self-hosted instance).  Exported as both
        ``LANGFUSE_BASE_URL`` and ``LANGFUSE_HOST``.
LangfuseSettings.title: LangfuseSettings

LifecycleSettings.description:
Deploy-lifecycle component settings.

Provides the base URL, API key, and per-request timeout for the
central-deploy lifecycle server.  Gated behind ``enabled`` — when
False (the default), no lifecycle tools are offered to the agent.
LifecycleSettings.title: LifecycleSettings

LlmSettings.description:
LLM endpoint settings for memory extraction.

Attributes:
    provider: Model provider e.g. ``custom`` for OpenRouter.
    model: Model name.
    endpoint: Base URL for API calls.
    api_key: API key.
LlmSettings.title: LlmSettings

MailSettings.description:
Mail board integration settings.

Direct HTTP access to the robotsix-auto-mail board server.  Gated
behind ``enabled`` — when False (the default), no mail tools are
offered to the agent.
MailSettings.title: MailSettings

MemoryEmbeddingSettings.description:
Embedding endpoint settings for cognee memory.

Attributes:
    provider: Embedding provider e.g. ``openai_compatible``.
    model: Embedding model name.
    endpoint: Embedding server URL (e.g. ``http://10.0.0.1:11434/v1``).
    dimensions: Embedding vector dimensions.
    api_key: API key.
    huggingface_tokenizer: HuggingFace tokenizer name.
MemoryEmbeddingSettings.title: MemoryEmbeddingSettings

MemoryLangfuseSettings.description:
Langfuse settings for the memory subsystem.

Separate credentials for a dedicated ``robotsix-chat-cognee`` Langfuse
project so internal LLM traffic (cognify, recall) lands in a
different project than the main-agent traces.

Attributes:
    public_key: Langfuse project public key.
    secret_key: Langfuse project secret key.
    host: Langfuse host URL.
MemoryLangfuseSettings.title: MemoryLangfuseSettings

MemoryLlmSettings.description:
LLM endpoint settings for cognee memory extraction.

Attributes:
    provider: Model provider e.g. ``custom`` for OpenRouter.
    model: Model name.
    endpoint: Base URL for API calls.
    api_key: API key.
MemoryLlmSettings.title: MemoryLlmSettings

MemorySettings.description:
Cognee-based long-term memory settings.

Persistent, cross-conversation episodic memory via an embedded cognee
instance running within the chat container.  Disabled by default
because cognee carries significant overhead — both in startup time
and per-turn LLM cost.

Attributes:
    enabled: Master switch.  When False, a no-op ``NullMemory`` is
        used instead of the full cognee pipeline.
    data_dir: Cognee store directory (should be on a persistent
        volume so memory survives restarts).
    recall_search_type: Cognee recall search type for querying stored
        memories (e.g. ``GRAPH_COMPLETION``, ``KEYWORD``).
    llm: The LLM endpoint settings used by cognee for extraction
        (cognify) and recall.
    embedding: The embedding endpoint settings.
    langfuse: Credentials for a separate Langfuse project that traces
        the cognee / LiteLLM pipeline independently from the main
        chat agent.
MemorySettings.title: MemorySettings

NotificationSettings.description:
Browser notification settings — lets the agent alert the user proactively.

When enabled, the agent gains a ``notify_user`` tool that publishes a
notification event to connected clients over the existing SSE channel
(EventBus).  The user's browser renders the event via the native
Notifications API.

Delivery only reaches clients that are currently connected — the
notification is silently dropped when no browser is listening.

Attributes:
    enabled: Master switch.  When ``False``, no notify_user tool is
        offered.
NotificationSettings.title: NotificationSettings

RefDocsSettings.description:
Reference-docs tool settings.

Read-only access to team GitHub repos.  Gated behind ``enabled``.
At minimum, ``repos`` must be non-empty when enabled.

Attributes:
    enabled: Master switch.
    repos: Allowlist of ``owner/name`` GitHub repos.
    ref: Default git ref/branch to read from.
    github_token: Optional GitHub PAT for private repos.
    base_url: Base URL for GitHub API.
    timeout: Per-request HTTP timeout.
RefDocsSettings.title: RefDocsSettings

RepoStudySettings.description:
Repo-study capability settings.

Temporary local repo snapshots (GitHub tarball, no git binary) the
agent can list, read, and regex-search before they expire.
Authentication for private repos reuses the ``direct_repo`` GitHub App
credentials; public repos need no auth.  Gated behind ``enabled``.

Attributes:
    enabled: Master switch.
    data_dir: Workspace directory on the persistent volume.
    ttl_minutes: Workspace lifetime before the automatic sweep.
    max_archive_bytes: Tarball download cap (64 MiB).
    max_extracted_bytes: Total uncompressed cap (256 MiB).
    max_read_bytes: Per-read file byte cap (200 KiB).
    timeout: Download HTTP timeout.
RepoStudySettings.title: RepoStudySettings

SelfReviewSettings.description:
Self-review tools settings.

A read-only digest of live conversation activity.  Gated behind
``enabled`` — when False (the default), no self-review tools are
offered to the agent.

Attributes:
    enabled: Master switch.
    recent_activity_limit: Maximum number of conversations returned by
        the ``read_recent_activity`` tool.
SelfReviewSettings.title: SelfReviewSettings

SubsessionsSettings.description:
Unified subsession system configuration.

Controls spawn limits, default model level, periodic run intervals,
and persistence for the subsession tree.

Attributes:
    max_concurrent: Maximum number of concurrently running subsessions
        across the entire server.
    max_depth: Maximum nesting depth for subsession spawning.
    default_model_level: Default model level assigned to subsessions
        when the spawner does not specify one.  Level 2 is the default
        choice for general work.
    min_interval_seconds: Minimum interval between periodic run
        executions.
    auto_stop_no_change_runs: Consecutive NO_CHANGE runs before
        auto-stopping a periodic subsession.
    store_path: Path to the subsession persistence JSON file.
    transcript_max_entries: Maximum transcript entries per subsession.
SubsessionsSettings.title: SubsessionsSettings

VersionCheckSettings.description:
Self-version-check tool settings.

Compares the running ``robotsix_chat.__version__`` against the latest
published GitHub release and warns the user when the deployment is
behind.  Gated behind ``enabled`` — when False (the default), no
version-check tool is offered to the agent.

Attributes:
    enabled: Master switch.
    repo: GitHub ``owner/name`` of the repository to check against.
        Required when enabled.
    github_token: Optional GitHub PAT to avoid rate limits.
    base_url: Base URL for GitHub API.
    timeout: Per-request HTTP timeout.
    cache_ttl: Seconds to cache the latest-release lookup before
        re-fetching.
VersionCheckSettings.title: VersionCheckSettings

description:
Application settings for robotsix-chat.

This is the single-source-of-truth settings model, loaded from the
JSON config file via ``robotsix-config``.  Every field has a sensible
default — the committed ``config/config.json`` mirrors those defaults
so operators only need to edit the fields they want to change.

There is **no YAML cascade and no env-var overlay** — the only
environment variable consumed for config is ``ROBOTSIX_CONFIG_FILE``,
which points to the JSON file.

Attributes:
    llmio_model_level: LLM capability level: ``1`` (cheapest), ``2``,
        ``3``, or ``4`` (best).
    summary_model_level: LLM capability level for the dedicated
        ``POST /summary`` extraction agent.  Default ``1`` (cheapest).
        Unlike ``llmio_model_level``, a missing API key for this
        level is not fatal — the server logs a warning and falls back
        to the keyless level 3 instead of refusing to start.
    llmio_api_key: OpenRouter API key.  Required for levels 1-2;
        ignored for keyless tiers 3-4.
    agent_instruction: System instruction for the agent.  Default is
        the pinned version from ``system_prompt.py``, tracked by
        ``SYSTEM_PROMPT_VERSION``.
    server_host: Host the server binds to.
    server_port: Port the server listens on.
    idle_timeout_minutes: Minutes of no user activity before
        auto-compaction. ``0`` disables.
    log_level: Python logging level.
    log_json_format: When ``True`` (default), logs are emitted as JSON
        suitable for structured log ingestion.  Set to ``False`` for
        human-readable console output during development.
    cors_allow_origins: Origins permitted to call ``/chat``
        cross-origin. ``[]`` (default) adds no CORS headers;
        ``["*"]`` allows any origin.
    correlation_id_header: HTTP header name used for the correlation /
        request-id (both inbound and outbound). Default ``X-Request-ID``.
    langfuse: Main-agent Langfuse observability credentials.
    feedback: Automated feedback analysis that files improvement
        tickets at compaction and session-end boundaries.
    max_images_per_message: Maximum number of images a client may attach to
        a single ``POST /chat`` request.  Default ``8``.
    max_image_bytes: Maximum decoded size (bytes) of a single attached
        image.  Default ``5_242_880`` (5 MiB).
    allowed_image_media_types: Allowed image MIME types.  Default
        ``["image/png", "image/jpeg", "image/gif", "image/webp"]``.
title: Settings
